title: STUDIO Science — Covalent Bonding in Water

shots.speech_text: Water looks simple — two hydrogens, one oxygen. But the bond holding them together is not a handoff of electrons. It is a share.

shots.speech_text: In a covalent bond, atoms pool valence electrons into a shared region. In water, oxygen pulls harder on those electrons, bending the molecule and making the O–H bonds polar.
